phases.title: University Application Documents
phases.description: Documents required to apply to Oslo universities (UiO, OsloMet, BI, etc.)
phases.info: Application Window: Non-EU/EEA applicants (Nepal) typically apply November 1 – December 1 for autumn intake. Apply directly on each university's portal. Most Oslo universities use their own system; some use Søknadsweb.

items.note: Original and scanned copy. Must be from a recognized institution.
Semester-wise mark sheets from your bachelor's university. Must show GPA/percentage.
National Examinations Board certificate. Required for Norwegian equivalency check.
NEB or Board certificate. Include transcript/marksheet.
Many Norwegian universities request a detailed course description from your bachelor's to check equivalency. Request from your university registrar.
Academic IELTS preferred. UiO typically requires 6.5; OsloMet requires 6.0. Check your specific program.
Accepted at most Oslo universities as an alternative to IELTS.
Letter from your bachelor's university confirming that your degree was taught in English. May waive IELTS at some universities.
500–1000 words explaining your background, why this program, and your career goals.
Academic and work experience. Use European CV format preferred by Norwegian universities.
From professors or employers. Should be on official letterhead, signed and dated. Some universities accept them uploaded online.
Passport must be valid for at least 18 months from intended travel date.
Required for design, architecture, journalism, or arts programs.
Required only for specific programs (e.g., BI Norwegian Business School MBA). Confirm with your target program.
Required for research-based masters. Usually 1,000–2,000 words outlining your research question and methodology.

phases.title: Document Attestation in Nepal
phases.description: Authentication process for Nepali documents — done before or after admission
phases.warn: Important: Nepal is not a member of the Hague Apostille Convention, so documents cannot be apostilled. Instead, you must go through a multi-step consular legalization process. Allow 2–4 weeks for the full attestation chain.

items.text: SLC/SEE Certificate verified by NEB (National Examinations Board)
items.note: NEB Office, Sanothimi, Bhaktapur. Bring original + photocopy.
Higher Secondary Education Board or NEB depending on year of completion.

items.text: Bachelor's Degree & Transcripts verified by Tribhuvan University (TU) Registrar / your university
items.note: Bring originals. TU Registrar Office, Kirtipur. Other universities (PU, KU, etc.) have their own registrar offices.
Request from your university's Dean's office or Registrar.

items.text: Educational documents attested by Ministry of Education, Science & Technology (MoEST)
items.note: Kesharmahal, Kathmandu. Required for all academic certificates after university-level verification.

sections.label: Step 3 — Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MoFA) Attestation

items.text: All attested documents legalized by Department of Consular Services, MoFA
items.note: MoFA, Tripureshwor, Kathmandu. This is the final Nepal-side stamp. Required for all academic, personal, and civil documents.

items.text: Birth Certificate attested by MoFA (if required by university)
items.note: Get it from your local banner office first, then municipality, then MoFA.

items.text: Citizenship Certificate copy attested by MoFA
items.note: Bring original citizenship + copy. Municipality verification may be needed first.

items.text: Notarize documents at a registered Notary Public Nepal (if originals cannot be sent)
items.note: For certified true copies when you cannot submit originals. Notarized copies must still go through MoFA.

items.text: Certified English translation of any Nepali-language documents
items.note: Norwegian authorities require documents in English or Norwegian. Use a certified translator. Most academic docs from Nepal are already in English.

phases.title: After University Admission Approval
phases.description: Documents to arrange once you receive your official offer letter from Oslo
phases.info: Congratulations on your offer! Once you receive your conditional or unconditional offer letter from your Oslo university, you move to the residence permit application. You apply online via UDI (udi.no) before submitting physical documents at VFS Global Kathmandu.

items.text: Official Admission / Offer Letter from Oslo University
items.note: Must state: full-time status, program name, duration, and start date. Without this, UDI application cannot proceed.

items.text: Tuition Fee Receipt / Confirmation of Payment
items.note: Proof that semester/annual tuition has been paid or waived (if applicable). Non-EU/EEA students pay tuition at most Oslo universities.
From the university's student housing (SiO for UiO/OsloMet) or a private rental agreement in Oslo showing your name and address.

items.text: Bank Statement showing min. NOK 166,859 (≈NPR 2.1 million) for 2025/26
items.note: Must be in your name. Funds should be present for 3–6 months (not freshly deposited). UDI requires this amount to be documented before granting a student permit.

items.text: Bank Letter / Certificate from your Nepali bank
items.note: Official letter on bank letterhead confirming account balance. Must be recent (within 1 month of application).

items.text: Sponsor's Bank Statement + Financial Guarantee Letter (if self-funded is insufficient)
items.note: If a parent or guardian is sponsoring you, include their bank statement + a notarized financial guarantee letter + proof of relationship (birth certificate).
From scholarship body (e.g., Quota Scheme, NORPART, etc.). Must specify amount and duration.

phases.title: VFS Global Submission (Kathmandu)
phases.description: Norway Student Residence Permit — documents to physically submit at VFS Kathmandu
phases.warn: Process: First register and pay online at udi.no, then book an appointment at VFS Kathmandu (Lagankhel, Lalitpur). Applications are forwarded to the Royal Norwegian Embassy, New Delhi for processing. Allow 48–72 hours transit time + up to 3 months processing. VFS service fee: 27 EUR (≈NPR 3,900). UDI fee: NOK 5,900 (~NPR 90,000).

items.note: Must have at least 18 months validity from application date. Must have at least 1 blank page. Bring old passports if any.
Taken within last 6 months. Unedited. Head should be 80% of the frame. Neutral expression, look at camera. One glued to form, one attached with paperclip (name on back).
Bring both sides. Certified copy may be needed.

items.text: UDI Online Application Summary / Confirmation printout
items.note: Generated after completing your application on udi.no. Print and bring to VFS appointment.
Auto-generated by UDI portal after submission. Must be signed and submitted at VFS.
Paid online at udi.no before booking VFS appointment.

items.text: Official Admission Letter from Oslo University
items.note: Must confirm full-time status, program, start date. Original or certified copy.
With all attestation stamps from Phase 2.
All semester transcripts, attested and in English.
Original TRF or score report. Not required to be attested.
From the university showing tuition for at least one semester/year is paid.
From a recognized Nepali bank. Must show account holder name, account number, and sufficient balance. Must be recent (within 1 month).
On bank letterhead, signed by bank manager. Must state current balance.

items.text: Sponsor's documents (if applicable): bank statement + guarantee letter + relationship proof
items.note: Parents' bank statement, their notarized financial guarantee letter, and your birth certificate or relationship certificate attested by MoFA.
Student housing letter (e.g., SiO confirmation) or private tenancy agreement in Oslo showing your name and address.

items.text: Covering / Personal Letter explaining study plans, funding, and return intent
items.note: 1 page. Explain why you chose Norway, your financial situation, and your plans after graduation. Strengthens your application.
Useful for identity verification. Bring along even if not specifically requested.
From Nepal Police, Naxal, Kathmandu. Some applications require it. Best to have it ready. Must be attested by MoFA.
Private travel/health insurance valid from arrival until registered with Norwegian National Insurance Scheme. UDI may require proof for first 12 months.

items.text: VFS Service Fee payment: 27 EUR (paid at VFS counter, cash or card)
items.note: VFS Global Kathmandu, Academy of Culinary Arts Building, Ground Floor, Mahalaxmisthan Marga, Lagankhel, Lalitpur.

items.text: Biometrics (fingerprints + photo) — provided in person at VFS
items.note: Mandatory physical presence at VFS Kathmandu. Norway Residence Case biometrics have been mandatory since March 2021. Applicants must appear in person.

phases.title: After Arrival in Oslo
phases.description: What to do once you land in Norway with your residence permit
phases.info: After you arrive: Your residence permit card will be waiting or will be issued by the local police in Oslo. Complete these steps within the first weeks of arrival.

items.text: Collect Residence Permit Card from Oslo Police / UDI
items.note: Book appointment at Oslo Police District (Grønland politistasjon or as directed by UDI letter).

items.text: Register Norwegian address at local municipality (Folkeregisteret)
items.note: Required within 8 days of arrival if staying more than 6 months.

items.text: Apply for Norwegian National ID Number (D-number or Personal Number) at Skatteetaten
items.note: Tax office (Skatt øst for Oslo). Bring passport + residence permit card. Needed for bank account and university enrollment.

items.text: Open Norwegian Bank Account (DNB, Sbanken, etc.)
items.note: Requires your residence permit card + D-number/Personal Number. Needed to receive SiO deposit refunds, scholarships, part-time wages.

items.text: Present original academic documents to university upon request
items.note: Universities may request originals at enrollment. Failing to present authentic originals can result in withdrawal of admission.

items.text: Pay Semester Fee (≈NOK 1,000) to student welfare org (SiO / Studentsamskipnad)
items.note: Required each semester. Gives access to student housing, healthcare, sports, and food services.

items.text: Register with Norwegian National Insurance Scheme (Helfo) for healthcare access
items.note: After getting your Personal Number. Entitles you to subsidized healthcare at Norwegian rates.
